behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real speech-to-text model diarization quality (speaker-label preservation), which a stubbed transcript would hide. The behavior text is about output correctness, not just API wiring. Rejected Workflow because the behavior only requires validating this single model-output property, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real multi-speaker meeting audio through the production speech-to-text API and assert the returned transcript includes correct speaker labels aligned to utterances used for attribution.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior targets deterministic parsing/mapping logic from transcript text into a structured schema; it can fail inside one local parser boundary without real I/O. Rejected Integration because behavior text does not require module handoff failure evidence; rejected Agentic/System/Workflow because no real model, network, or multi-step agent pipeline is required by this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Feed transcript fixtures containing explicit assignments into the parsing function/object and assert exact extracted fields (`assignee`, `task_text`) in the summary-input structure.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM summarization/output content (coverage of assigned attendees). A mocked LLM response would trivially pass and hide missed assignments. Rejected Workflow because this can be validated with one real summarization step from a transcript fixture; behavior text does not require multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a transcript with known attendee-task assignments to the real summarization model and assert the produced structured summary includes at least one action item per assigned attendee.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly focuses on routing logic after decisions are already identified; this is local schema-mapping behavior. Rejected Integration because no concrete cross-module failure is required by the text; rejected Agentic/System/Workflow because behavior does not require real model judgment, external infra, or multi-step sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the decision-routing function with input containing pre-identified decision items and assert they are placed only in the `decisions` schema field, not narrative buckets.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This checks model output quality: whether the summarizer identifies and places key decisions correctly. Stubbed model output would hide extraction/placement failures. Rejected Workflow because behavior can be validated in a single real summarization pass from transcript input; no explicit multi-agentic chain requirement in text.
behaviors.test_description: Send transcripts with known decision statements to the real LLM summarizer and assert those decisions are present in the `decisions` section and absent from narrative-only placement.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires multi-step sequence: split -> per-segment processing -> merge. Per-segment processing includes real model calls, and correctness depends on sequential orchestration across those agentic steps. Rejected Agentic because single-step model testing cannot expose cross-segment handoff/merge failures required by this text.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through production segmentation and sequential processing, then verify merged output preserves cross-segment continuity and expected structured sections in one final summary.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly end-to-end and includes multiple agentic steps (transcribe + summarize) plus orchestration/delivery. Real pipeline interactions are required to catch failures that mocks hide. Rejected lower levels because behavior text requires full pipeline completion on a real recording without manual intervention.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the production pipeline on a real meeting audio file from upload through delivery and assert successful autonomous completion with valid structured output payload.
